City Engineer Ralston last night
submitted to the council detailed
opinion ON grade separation cover
ing every phase of the pending n.
p. discussion. His plans contem-
plate Encouragement Of railway
facilities in this city and appeared
so near the proper solution, II pos
sible of accomplishment, that the
council will take it up by commit
tee with the railroads concerned.


In brief his plan is that the g..
n and N. p. tracks be connected
south of Greene and Walton SITS.
and new freight and warehouse
district be created in that territory
from the junction to Sprague av.
and Ivory st. This scheme contem-
plates a common user of the track
age for all lines, the o. R. & n.
connecting at the Crestline and
Olive av. intersection. and all 9th
err roads from the East finding easy
entrance from their respective loca-
tons. A union passenger depot Is
also provided for, either at the
east tllu vi tHC cpia d'. tiSSeiS
or at the site of the present n. p,
freight depot on Division st. A
slight deviation in streetcar routes
at this point would be necessary.


This plan, the engineer argues,
would permit the three Hill lines a
common track outlet west, to be Sc
cured by a 100 foot tunnel
through Cannon hill, beginning
near the East Sprague trestle and
terminating at the Hangman creek
bank in Stafford's addition, the
rails to be carried on across the
valley on a high trestle.


This would involve enormous
cost and necessitate abandonment
OF the present n. p. right of way
through the city, but Mr.. Ralston
